sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board changed after pressing SPACE. Let me compare the before and after frames to see what changed. Looking at the current image vs the previous one, it seems like the white block below the purple frame has changed - now there's purple on top and white below within that lower block. Let me check the diff properly.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after SPACE press
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
after = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']

print("BEFORE nodes:")
for n in before:
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} hash={n['hash']} boundary={n['boundary']}")

print("\nAFTER nodes:")
for n in after:
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} hash={n['hash']} boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Find what changed
before_hashes = {n['hash']: n for n in before}
after_hashes = {n['hash']: n for n in after}

print("\nNew hashes in after:", set(after_hashes.keys()) - set(before_hashes.keys()))
print("Removed hashes:", set(before_hashes.keys()) - set(after_hashes.keys()))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: USER PROMPT

If `MOUSE` is valid, use integer `row` and `col`.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Let me analyze what changed:
Before SPACE:
- id=13: W (white) pixels=100, boundary=[[34, 27], [34, 36], [43, 36], [43, 27]] - 9x9 white block
- id=14: c (charcoal) pixels=64 - bottom strip
After SPACE:
- id=13: p (purple) pixels=50, boundary=[[34, 27], [34, 36], [38, 36], [38, 27]] - 5x9 purple block (top half)
- id=14: W (white) pixels=50, boundary=[[39, 27], [39, 36], [43, 36], [43, 27]] - 5x9 white block (bottom half)
- id=15: c (charcoal) pixels=63 - bottom strip (slightly different)
So SPACE caused the white 9x9 block to split into a purple top half (5x9) and white bottom half (5x9). This matches the template shown in the top-left panel (white on top, purple on bottom... wait, no - the template shows white on top and purple on bottom).
Wait, let me re-check. The top-left panel has:
- id=5: W pixels=50 (white block) 
- id=9: p pixels=50 (purple block)
Looking at boundaries:
- id=5: [[3, 3], [3, 12], [7, 12], [7, 3]] - rows 3-7, so white is on top
- id=9: [[8, 3], [8, 12], [12, 12], [12, 3]] - rows 8-12, so purple is below
So the template shows: white on top, purple on bottom.
After SPACE, the lower block became: purple on top (id=13), white on bottom (id=14). This is the OPPOSITE of the template!
Wait, let me re-examine. The template in top-left is white top, purple bottom. After SPACE, the block is purple top, white bottom. That's reversed from the template.
Hmm, but looking at the current image, it shows purple on top and white on bottom in the lower block. The template in the top-left shows white on top and purple on bottom.
So the goal might be to make the lower block match the template pattern (white on top, purple on bottom). Currently it's reversed.
Let me try SPACE again to see if it cycles or reverses.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# SPACE changed the white block to purple-top/white-bottom. 
# Template shows white-top/purple-bottom. Let me try SPACE again to see if it cycles.
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
